prompt: Image-to-video transition prompt. Use the generated image for text id 1 as the first frame and the generated image for text id 2 as the final frame. Create a smooth cinematic landscape 16:9 transition in POV anime style. Begin with a gentle push-in toward the faceless student's open notebook and the physically printed question on the paper. The student's faceless hand slowly moves the pencil across the storyboard, then turns one notebook page. Follow the page edge with a natural match movement: the cream paper briefly fills the frame and reveals the warm Elyon entrance lobby from the second image, where the transparent acrylic plaque is already resting on the reception counter. Keep all text physically attached to its original object while it is visible; let the notebook text leave the frame naturally with the page turn, and never morph text into the air. Preserve the yellow-and-blue Elyon architecture, warm light, realistic hand motion, paper texture, and acrylic reflections. Every human figure remains faceless: no eyes, nose, mouth, or facial details. No new readable words, no text morphing, no floating typography, no subtitles, no logos, no watermarks, and no visible faces. Critical text continuity: keep the complete source text fully visible and legible on its original physical object until the target text has already appeared and is clearly legible on its own physical object. Create a brief 0.3–0.5 second overlap where both texts are visible at the same time. Do not erase, fade out, crop, blur, or morph the source text before the target text appears. Make this fully seamless and uninterrupted: use one continuous tracking shot with a smooth physical morph between scenes. The same faceless student visibly walks naturally from the source scene toward the target scene, viewed from behind or side-back; keep the same clothing silhouette, body movement, and hand motion continuous, and never reveal a face. There must be no cut at all: no hard cut, no jump cut, and no slideshow. Audio policy: Absolutely no music of any kind: no soundtrack, melody, beat, singing, instruments, musical ambience, or jingle. Keep the transition audio silent; add the voice-over separately in post-production.
